input: On Tuesday, NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory confirmed that the Perseverance rover successfully drilled its eighth rock sample from the Jezero Crater floor on Mars. The cylindrical core, roughly the size of a piece of chalk, was sealed inside a titanium tube and stored in the rover's belly cache for future retrieval by the Mars Sample Return mission. Scientists believe the sample may contain ancient microbial biosignatures because the rock was extracted from a sedimentary layer deposited when a river delta fed into the crater roughly 3.5 billion years ago. The drilling operation took approximately 55 minutes, with the rover's robotic arm exerting controlled rotational force to avoid fracturing the brittle mudstone. Summarize this passage in 1-2 sentences.
expected: NASA's Perseverance rover drilled its eighth rock sample from a sedimentary layer in Mars's Jezero Crater, sealing it for future return to Earth. Scientists think the sample may contain ancient microbial biosignatures from a 3.5-billion-year-old river delta deposit.
rubric: Summary must mention: Perseverance rover, Jezero Crater, eighth sample, sealed in titanium tube, and the 3.5-billion-year-old river delta. Must NOT hallucinate a launch date, a specific microbe name, or claim the sample has already been returned to Earth.

input: The Appellate Division of the New York Supreme Court ruled 4-1 on March 12 that Landlord Greenfield Properties LLC breached the implied warranty of habitability by failing to remediate a persistent mold infestation in Units 4A through 4F of 312 West 116th Street over a 14-month period despite repeated written complaints from tenants. The court upheld the lower court's order requiring Greenfield to abate the mold within 60 days and to reimburse affected tenants a total of $87,400 in rent abatements. Justice Delgado dissented, arguing that the tenants failed to grant reasonable access for inspection on two occasions. Summarize this passage in 1-2 sentences.
expected: A New York appellate court ruled 4-1 that Greenfield Properties breached the warranty of habitability by ignoring a 14-month mold problem, upholding an order for 60-day remediation and $87,400 in rent refunds. One justice dissented on the ground that tenants did not always allow inspection access.
rubric: Summary must include: the 4-1 ruling, Greenfield Properties, breach of implied warranty of habitability, mold infestation, 60-day abatement, and the $87,400 figure. Must NOT hallucinate a different court, fabricate additional monetary penalties, or omit the dissent.

input: Cisco released firmware version 17.9.4 for the Catalyst 9300 series switches to patch CVE-2024-20356, a critical heap-overflow vulnerability in the HTTPS management interface that allows unauthenticated remote code execution. The CVSS 3.1 base score is 9.8. Administrators are advised to upgrade immediately or, as a temporary workaround, disable the HTTPS management interface and restrict management-plane access through an out-of-band network. The vulnerability was discovered by a researcher at Trellix and responsibly disclosed in January 2024. Summarize this passage in 1-2 sentences.
expected: Cisco patched CVE-2024-20356, a critical (CVSS 9.8) heap-overflow vulnerability in the Catalyst 9300 switches' HTTPS management interface that permits unauthenticated remote code execution. Administrators should upgrade to firmware 17.9.4 or disable the HTTPS interface as a workaround.
rubric: Summary must mention: CVE-2024-20356, Catalyst 9300, heap-overflow, CVSS 9.8, remote code execution, and firmware 17.9.4. Must NOT hallucinate a different CVE number, claim the vulnerability was in SSH, or invent a Cisco product name.

input: In the final three kilometres of Stage 17 of the Tour de France, Danish cyclist Jonas Vingegaard attacked on the Col de la Loze's 24-percent gradient, dropping race leader Tadej Pogačar and opening a gap of 47 seconds by the summit finish. The stage win moved Vingegaard from third to second in the general classification, now trailing Pogačar by only 38 seconds. Pogačar later said his legs cramped on the steepest ramps and acknowledged that the final week would be decisive. Team Jumbo-Visma's directeur sportif called the ride one of the most explosive climbing performances in modern Grand Tour history. Summarize this passage in 1-2 sentences.
expected: Jonas Vingegaard won Stage 17 of the Tour de France by attacking on the Col de la Loze's steep gradient, gaining 47 seconds on race leader Tadej Pogačar and moving to within 38 seconds in the general classification. Pogačar attributed his loss to leg cramps on the steepest section.
rubric: Summary must mention: Vingegaard, Stage 17, Col de la Loze, 47-second gap, Pogačar, and the 38-second GC deficit. Must NOT hallucinate a different stage number, claim Vingegaard took the yellow jersey, or invent a finishing time.

input: A peer-reviewed study published in Nature Climate Change analysed 40 years of satellite imagery and found that 68 percent of the world's large lakes experienced statistically significant declines in surface water area between 1984 and 2023, with the Aral Sea, Lake Chad, and the Salton Sea showing reductions exceeding 50 percent. The researchers attributed the shrinkage primarily to a combination of upstream agricultural irrigation withdrawals and rising evaporation rates driven by higher regional temperatures. Lakes in arid and semi-arid regions were disproportionately affected. The team used Landsat and Sentinel-2 multispectral imagery processed through Google Earth Engine. Summarize this passage in 1-2 sentences.
expected: A Nature Climate Change study of 40 years of satellite data found that 68 percent of the world's large lakes have significantly shrunk since 1984, with the Aral Sea, Lake Chad, and Salton Sea losing over 50 percent of their surface area. Agricultural irrigation and rising temperatures were identified as the primary causes.
rubric: Summary must include: 68 percent figure, the three named lakes, 50-percent-plus reductions, and the two causes (irrigation, rising temperatures). Must NOT hallucinate different lake names, invent a percentage, or claim the study appeared in a different journal.

input: Amazon Web Services announced that its new eu-south-2 region in Aragón, Spain is now generally available, comprising three Availability Zones powered entirely by renewable energy purchased through local power-purchase agreements. The region offers 82 AWS services at launch, including EC2, S3, RDS, and Lambda. AWS said the region was built in response to demand from European financial-services and public-sector customers who require data residency within the EU. Pricing for compute instances in eu-south-2 is approximately 5 percent higher than in the eu-west-1 (Ireland) region. Summarize this passage in 1-2 sentences.
expected: AWS launched its eu-south-2 region in Aragón, Spain, with three Availability Zones powered by renewable energy and 82 services available at launch. The region targets European customers needing EU data residency, with compute pricing about 5 percent above eu-west-1.
rubric: Summary must mention: eu-south-2, Aragón Spain, three Availability Zones, renewable energy, 82 services, and the 5-percent pricing premium. Must NOT hallucinate a different country, claim a different number of AZs, or state that the region is still in preview.

input: During an archaeological excavation at Vindolanda, a Roman fort near Hadrian's Wall in northern England, researchers unearthed a wooden writing tablet dating to approximately 105 CE. The tablet contains a personal letter from a soldier named Masculus to his commanding officer requesting additional leave to visit his ailing mother in Gaul. Infrared imaging revealed faded ink traces confirming the tablet was written in cursive Latin script. The find is significant because fewer than 500 such tablets have been recovered from the entire Roman Empire, and personal-leave requests are exceptionally rare among surviving military correspondence. Summarize this passage in 1-2 sentences.
expected: Archaeologists at the Roman fort of Vindolanda near Hadrian's Wall discovered a wooden writing tablet from around 105 CE containing a soldier's personal letter requesting leave to visit his sick mother in Gaul. The find is notable because personal-leave requests are extremely rare among the fewer than 500 surviving Roman writing tablets.
rubric: Summary must mention: Vindolanda, Hadrian's Wall, 105 CE, soldier named Masculus, leave request, mother in Gaul, and the rarity (fewer than 500 tablets). Must NOT hallucinate a different date, claim the tablet was papyrus, or state the request was denied.

input: Tesla's Q3 2024 earnings report showed revenue of $25.2 billion, up 8 percent year-over-year, beating analyst consensus of $24.1 billion. Automotive gross margin improved to 19.8 percent from 16.3 percent in the prior quarter, driven by lower raw-material costs and manufacturing efficiencies at Gigafactory Texas. However, the energy-generation and storage segment reported a $42 million operating loss, attributed to warranty reserves on older Powerwall units. Free cash flow reached $2.7 billion, and Tesla ended the quarter with $33.6 billion in cash and equivalents. Summarize this passage in 1-2 sentences.
expected: Tesla reported Q3 2024 revenue of $25.2 billion, an 8 percent year-over-year increase that beat analyst estimates, with automotive gross margin rising to 19.8 percent. The energy segment posted a $42 million operating loss, while free cash flow reached $2.7 billion.
rubric: Summary must include: $25.2B revenue, 8-percent growth, 19.8-percent automotive margin, $42M energy-segment loss, and $2.7B free cash flow. Must NOT hallucinate a different revenue figure, claim the energy segment was profitable, or invent an EPS number.

input: Researchers at ETH Zurich demonstrated a novel solid-state lithium-sulfur battery cell that achieved an energy density of 540 watt-hours per kilogram at the cell level, roughly double that of today's commercial lithium-ion cells. The cell uses a lithium-metal anode, a sulfur-carbon composite cathode, and a ceramic Li₆PS₅Cl electrolyte. In lab tests the cell retained 88 percent of its initial capacity after 1,000 charge-discharge cycles at a rate of 0.5C. The researchers noted that manufacturing scale-up remains a challenge because the ceramic electrolyte requires sintering at 550 degrees Celsius under inert-gas atmosphere. Summarize this passage in 1-2 sentences.
expected: ETH Zurich researchers built a solid-state lithium-sulfur battery achieving 540 Wh/kg energy density, about double current lithium-ion cells, with 88 percent capacity retention after 1,000 cycles. Scale-up is challenging because the ceramic electrolyte must be sintered at 550°C in an inert atmosphere.
rubric: Summary must mention: ETH Zurich, solid-state lithium-sulfur, 540 Wh/kg, 88 percent retention, 1,000 cycles, and the sintering challenge. Must NOT hallucinate a commercial release date, claim the battery is already in production, or invent a different energy-density figure.

input: The U.S. Federal Reserve held its benchmark federal-funds rate steady at the 5.25-to-5.50-percent range at its September 2024 meeting, marking the eighth consecutive pause. In the post-meeting statement, Chair Jerome Powell said inflation had eased to 2.5 percent on a 12-month PCE basis but remained above the 2-percent target. Powell indicated that the committee would need to see further sustained progress before considering rate cuts, and the updated dot plot showed a median projection of two 25-basis-point cuts in 2025. Treasury yields fell modestly after the announcement. Summarize this passage in 1-2 sentences.
expected: The Federal Reserve kept the federal-funds rate at 5.25-5.50 percent for the eighth straight meeting in September 2024, with Chair Powell noting inflation at 2.5 percent remains above the 2-percent target. The updated dot plot projected two 25-basis-point rate cuts in 2025.
rubric: Summary must mention: 5.25-5.50 percent rate, eighth consecutive hold, 2.5 percent PCE inflation, and the dot-plot projection of two cuts in 2025. Must NOT hallucinate a rate cut, claim inflation hit the 2-percent target, or fabricate a Treasury-yield figure.

input: Marine biologists from the Monterey Bay Aquarium Research Institute captured rare footage of a deep-sea anglerfish, Melanocetus johnsonii, at a depth of 3,200 metres in the Monterey Canyon using the remotely operated vehicle Doc Ricketts. The female specimen measured 14 centimetres in length and had a bioluminescent lure, or esca, that pulsed with blue light at irregular intervals. Two parasitic dwarf males, each roughly one centimetre long, were fused to the female's ventral surface, a mating strategy unique to ceratioid anglerfish. This is only the third time M. johnsonii has been observed alive in its natural habitat. Summarize this passage in 1-2 sentences.
expected: MBARI filmed a rare deep-sea anglerfish (Melanocetus johnsonii) at 3,200 metres in the Monterey Canyon, marking only the third live observation of the species. The 14-cm female had a pulsing bioluminescent lure and two parasitic dwarf males fused to her body.
rubric: Summary must mention: MBARI, Melanocetus johnsonii, 3,200-metre depth, Monterey Canyon, bioluminescent lure, two parasitic males, and third live observation. Must NOT hallucinate a different species, claim the fish was captured, or invent a different depth.

input: The European Parliament voted 412-153 on June 14 to adopt the Artificial Intelligence Act, the world's first comprehensive legal framework for regulating AI systems. The regulation classifies AI applications into four risk tiers — unacceptable, high, limited, and minimal — and bans real-time biometric surveillance in public spaces except for narrowly defined law-enforcement scenarios. High-risk systems, such as those used in hiring or credit scoring, must undergo conformity assessments and maintain human oversight. Companies face fines of up to 35 million euros or 7 percent of global annual turnover, whichever is higher. The act takes full effect 24 months after publication in the Official Journal. Summarize this passage in 1-2 sentences.
expected: The European Parliament adopted the AI Act by a 412-153 vote, creating the first comprehensive AI regulation with four risk tiers and a near-total ban on real-time public biometric surveillance. Non-compliant companies face fines up to 35 million euros or 7 percent of global turnover, with the act taking effect two years after publication.
rubric: Summary must include: 412-153 vote, AI Act, four risk tiers, ban on real-time biometric surveillance, fine of 35M euros or 7 percent of turnover, and 24-month effective date. Must NOT hallucinate a different vote count, claim the act is already in force, or invent additional banned use cases.

input: In the opening chapter of the novella, twelve-year-old Sayo discovers a sealed iron door behind the crumbling plaster of her grandmother's farmhouse cellar in rural Tōhoku. When she pries the door open with a crowbar, a gust of cold air smelling of wet cedar rushes out, and a narrow stone staircase descends into darkness. Her grandmother, watching from the top step, warns her that the stairs lead to the old silver-mine tunnels that were sealed after a collapse killed seven miners in 1923. Sayo hesitates but notices fresh scratch marks on the stone walls, as though someone — or something — has been climbing up recently. Summarize this passage in 1-2 sentences.
expected: Twelve-year-old Sayo finds a hidden iron door in her grandmother's farmhouse cellar in Tōhoku that leads to old silver-mine tunnels sealed since a deadly 1923 collapse. Fresh scratch marks on the tunnel walls suggest something has recently been coming up through the staircase.
rubric: Summary must mention: Sayo, sealed iron door, grandmother's farmhouse, silver-mine tunnels, 1923 collapse, and the fresh scratch marks. Must NOT hallucinate Sayo's age incorrectly, claim she descended the stairs, or add characters not in the passage.

input: A clinical trial published in The Lancet enrolled 4,200 adults aged 50-75 with type 2 diabetes across 38 sites in the United Kingdom and Germany. Participants receiving the experimental GLP-1/GIP dual-agonist tirzepatide at a 15-mg weekly dose achieved a mean HbA1c reduction of 2.4 percentage points from a baseline of 8.9 percent after 52 weeks, compared with 1.1 percentage points for the semaglutide 1-mg comparator arm. The tirzepatide group also lost an average of 12.8 kilograms of body weight versus 5.9 kilograms in the comparator. Gastrointestinal adverse events, primarily nausea, occurred in 28 percent of tirzepatide recipients versus 18 percent of semaglutide recipients. Summarize this passage in 1-2 sentences.
expected: A 4,200-patient Lancet trial found that 15-mg tirzepatide reduced HbA1c by 2.4 percentage points and body weight by 12.8 kg over 52 weeks, outperforming semaglutide 1 mg on both measures. Nausea was more common with tirzepatide (28 percent versus 18 percent).
rubric: Summary must include: 4,200 participants, tirzepatide 15 mg, HbA1c reduction of 2.4 points, 12.8 kg weight loss, semaglutide comparator, and the nausea rates. Must NOT hallucinate a different trial size, invent a mortality endpoint, or claim FDA approval.

input: During the 2024 Michelin Guide ceremony in Paris, the committee awarded a third star to Kei Kobayashi's restaurant Kei in the 1st arrondissement, making him the first Japanese-born chef to hold three Michelin stars for a restaurant in France. Kobayashi's tasting menu, priced at 390 euros, blends classical French technique with Japanese seasonal ingredients such as yuzu kosho and shiso. The committee cited extraordinary consistency over a decade and a signature dish of truffle-layered mille-feuille with smoked dashi butter. Two restaurants were demoted from two stars to one: Le Pré Catelan in the Bois de Boulogne and La Maison des Bois in Manigod. Summarize this passage in 1-2 sentences.
expected: Chef Kei Kobayashi became the first Japanese-born chef to earn three Michelin stars in France at his Paris restaurant Kei, recognized for blending French technique with Japanese ingredients. The 2024 ceremony also saw Le Pré Catelan and La Maison des Bois demoted from two stars to one.
rubric: Summary must mention: Kei Kobayashi, first Japanese-born chef, three Michelin stars, France, and the two demoted restaurants. Must NOT hallucinate additional promotions, claim a different nationality, or invent a Michelin rating for another restaurant.

input: Mumbai-based fintech startup ZestPay closed a $120 million Series C round led by Sequoia Capital India, with participation from Tiger Global and existing investor Accel. The company's unified payments interface processes an average of 18 million transactions per day across 340,000 merchant endpoints in India. CEO Priya Narayan stated that the funds will be used to expand into Southeast Asian markets — starting with Indonesia and Vietnam — and to build a lending product targeting micro-merchants with annual revenues below 5 lakh rupees. ZestPay's post-money valuation is reported at $1.1 billion, making it India's newest fintech unicorn. Summarize this passage in 1-2 sentences.
expected: ZestPay raised $120 million in a Sequoia-led Series C, valuing the Mumbai fintech at $1.1 billion and making it a unicorn. The funds will finance expansion into Indonesia and Vietnam and a lending product for micro-merchants.
rubric: Summary must include: ZestPay, $120M Series C, Sequoia lead, $1.1B valuation, unicorn status, and expansion into Indonesia and Vietnam. Must NOT hallucinate a different valuation, claim the company is publicly traded, or invent additional investors.

input: The City of Copenhagen announced that its municipal bus fleet has completed a full transition to battery-electric vehicles, retiring its last 14 diesel buses on September 30. The fleet now consists of 425 BYD K9-series 12-metre buses, each with a 324-kWh lithium-iron-phosphate battery pack providing a certified range of 280 kilometres per charge. The city invested 2.4 billion Danish kroner over five years in buses and depot charging infrastructure. Transport Mayor Sophie Hæstorp Andersen said the transition has cut annual fleet CO₂ emissions by an estimated 25,000 tonnes compared with the 2019 diesel baseline. Summarize this passage in 1-2 sentences.
expected: Copenhagen fully electrified its municipal bus fleet with 425 BYD K9 battery-electric buses after a 2.4-billion-kroner, five-year investment, retiring its last diesel buses on September 30. The transition is estimated to reduce annual fleet CO₂ emissions by 25,000 tonnes versus 2019 levels.
rubric: Summary must include: Copenhagen, 425 BYD K9 buses, full electrification, 2.4 billion kroner investment, September 30 diesel retirement, and 25,000-tonne CO₂ reduction. Must NOT hallucinate a different bus manufacturer, claim the fleet runs on hydrogen, or invent a different emissions figure.

input: The board of directors of Kyoto Animation Co., Ltd. announced the appointment of Mikiko Watanabe as the company's new president, succeeding Hideaki Hatta who is retiring after 40 years with the studio. Watanabe, 52, has served as executive producer on critically acclaimed titles including Violet Evergarden, A Silent Voice, and the Free! franchise. In a press conference, she outlined plans to open a satellite studio in Osaka by mid-2025 and to double the studio's annual feature-film output from one to two titles starting in 2026. She also confirmed that the studio's next theatrical release, a fantasy original titled The Loom of Stars, is scheduled for December 2025. Summarize this passage in 1-2 sentences.
expected: Kyoto Animation appointed Mikiko Watanabe as president, succeeding retiring 40-year veteran Hideaki Hatta, with plans to open an Osaka satellite studio and double annual feature-film output. The studio's next film, The Loom of Stars, is slated for December 2025.
rubric: Summary must mention: Mikiko Watanabe, new president, Kyoto Animation, Hideaki Hatta retiring, Osaka satellite studio, doubled film output, and The Loom of Stars. Must NOT hallucinate titles Watanabe did not produce, claim the studio was acquired, or invent a different release date.

input: A controlled experiment at the University of British Columbia tracked 240 undergraduate students over one academic semester to measure the effect of handwriting versus laptop note-taking on long-term retention. Students were randomly assigned to two groups of 120 and attended identical introductory psychology lectures. The handwriting group scored a mean of 78.3 percent on a cumulative final exam compared with 71.6 percent for the laptop group, a difference that was statistically significant at p < 0.01 after controlling for GPA and prior coursework. The researchers hypothesised that handwriting forces greater encoding effort because students must paraphrase rather than transcribe verbatim. Summarize this passage in 1-2 sentences.
expected: A UBC study of 240 undergraduates found that those who took notes by hand scored significantly higher on a final exam (78.3 vs. 71.6 percent, p < 0.01) than laptop note-takers. The researchers attribute the advantage to the deeper cognitive processing required by handwriting.
rubric: Summary must include: UBC, 240 students, handwriting vs. laptop, 78.3 vs. 71.6 percent scores, p < 0.01, and the encoding-effort hypothesis. Must NOT hallucinate a different sample size, claim no significant difference, or invent additional measured outcomes.

input: SpaceX's Falcon Heavy rocket successfully deployed the GOES-U geostationary weather satellite into a transfer orbit from Launch Complex 39A at Kennedy Space Center on June 25 at 5:26 PM EDT. The satellite, built by Lockheed Martin for NOAA, carries four instruments including the Advanced Baseline Imager and the Geostationary Lightning Mapper. GOES-U will provide real-time hurricane tracking, severe-storm warnings, and space-weather monitoring for the Western Hemisphere once it reaches its operational slot at 137.2 degrees west longitude in approximately four months. Both side boosters landed successfully at Cape Canaveral's Landing Zones 1 and 2, while the centre core was expended as planned. Summarize this passage in 1-2 sentences.
expected: SpaceX's Falcon Heavy launched the NOAA GOES-U weather satellite into transfer orbit from Kennedy Space Center on June 25, with both side boosters landing successfully. GOES-U will provide hurricane tracking and severe-weather monitoring from geostationary orbit at 137.2 degrees west once operational in about four months.
rubric: Summary must mention: Falcon Heavy, GOES-U, NOAA, Kennedy Space Center, June 25, side boosters landing, and the 137.2-degree operational slot. Must NOT hallucinate a Falcon 9 launch, claim the centre core was recovered, or invent a different satellite name.
